channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sindh Government Advances March Salaries and Pensions for Eid Preparations
item.description: The Sindh government has decided to release the salaries, allowances, and pensions of Muslim employees and pensioners earlier than usual for the month of March. This advance payment is scheduled for M...

item.title: President Ramaphosa to Enhance South Africa-Brazil Cooperation on State Visit
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to undertake a State Visit to Brazil from 9 to 10 March 2026, at the invitation of Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. The visit aims to strengthen the hist...

item.title: Historical Perspective on Lok Sabha Speaker Impeachment and Om Birla
item.description: The news article discusses the no-confidence motion concerning Om Birla, the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, and explores whether a Speaker of India's lower house has ever been impeached in the past. While ...

item.title: ICPC Links Luxury Properties in Egypt Worth Millions of Dollars to Ex-Kaduna Governor Nasir El-Rufai Amid Corruption Probe
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) don trace several luxury real estate properties for Egypt come link dem to former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, ...

item.title: Sri Lanka grants one-month humanitarian visas to rescued Iranian sailors
item.description: Sri Lanka has announced that it will provide one-month visas free of cost to Iranian sailors rescued after attacks on their ships. The decision was confirmed by Minister of Public Security Ananda Wije...

item.title: Governor Douye Diri Honoured for Advancing Sports Infrastructure in Bayelsa
item.description: Bayelsa State Governor, Douye Diri, don dey gather plenty praise for him efforts wey dey transform sports infrastructure for him state. Last Friday, during the second edition of the Newstap/SWAN Five ...

item.title: Matildas held to 3-3 draw by South Korea, finish second in group at Women’s Asian Cup
item.description: Australia’s Women’s Asian Cup campaign received a wake-up call as the Matildas managed only a 3-3 draw against South Korea in front of 60,279 fans at Stadium Australia. The draw meant South Korea topp...

item.title: French Ambassador Praises Nigerian Culture, Hospitality and Art
item.description: The French Ambassador to Nigeria, Marc Fonbaustier, don talk say him dey really impressed with the culture and heritage of Nigeria. For one-on-one interview with Premium Times, he talk say na the mix ...

item.title: Safari Rally Teams Begin Reconnaissance on Challenging Naivasha Tracks
item.description: The 2026 WRC Safari Rally Kenya kicked off its preparations with crews hitting the tough terrain around Naivasha for their first reconnaissance runs. Organisers have warned teams to brace for slippery...

item.title: Government allocates funds to improve Kariakoo Market for small-scale traders
item.description: The Tanzanian government has earmarked 28 billion shillings to improve Kariakoo Market in Dar es Salaam, focusing on creating a better business environment and enhancing infrastructure. Ilala District...

item.title: Lok Sabha adjourned after opposition protests over West Asia situation
item.description: On Monday, the Lok Sabha proceedings were adjourned till 3 pm due to disruptions caused by opposition members demanding a full debate on the ongoing West Asia crisis. External Affairs Minister S. Jais...

item.title: Australia implements strict online age verification, prompting surge in VPN use
item.description: On March 9, 2026, Australia implemented new nationwide online age restrictions, causing a spike in VPN downloads as users sought to bypass blocked content. These regulations prohibit teenagers from ac...

item.title: Iran Confirms Death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei After US-Israel Strikes, Sparking US-China Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: Iranian state television don confirm the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who don hold power since 1989 and na 86 years old. The announcement come early morning with black banner for mo...

item.title: Travel Moments That Transform You Beyond Words
item.description: The article explores travel experiences that are so extraordinary they cannot be fully conveyed through words. Ordinary descriptions fall short when attempting to communicate the sensory and emotional...

item.title: Jaishankar Explains India’s Response to Middle East Conflict Amid Iran Tensions
item.description: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar addressed Rajya Sabha, explaining India’s approach to the escalating conflict in the Middle East. He emphasised that India advocates for dialogue and diplomacy ...

item.title: Police launch full investigation after GTA synagogue shootings
item.description: Toronto police have vowed to conduct a thorough and relentless investigation following a shooting at a synagogue in the Greater Toronto Area (GTA). Authorities are taking the incident very seriously a...

item.title: Carlton reportedly explored approach to Collingwood coach Craig McRae amid Voss pressure
item.description: Last year, Carlton Football Club reportedly made a significant approach to Collingwood premiership coach Craig McRae, according to Channel 7 journalist Caroline Wilson. The move came at a time when Ca...

item.title: KSE-100 Index Suffers Major Loss Amid Global Oil Spike and Regional Tensions
item.description: On Monday, Pakistan’s stock market experienced a serious downturn as the KSE-100 index fell by 11,015.96 points, closing at 146,480.14, a decline of almost 7 percent from the previous close of 157,496...

item.title: Early Hearing Screening for Newborns in South Africa Remains Limited
item.description: In South Africa, thousands of babies are born with hearing loss every year, yet most are only diagnosed after the crucial early years for language development. Hearing loss is the most common sensory ...

item.title: Victor Osimhen Scores Winner in Istanbul Derby, Earns High Praise as Top Striker in Turkey from Former Besiktas Official
item.description: Victor Osimhen don show say him be the main man for Turkish football as e lead Galatasaray to 1-0 victory against their big rival Besiktas for the hot Istanbul derby on Saturday. The Nigerian striker ...
